description: Represent UE Blueprint, C++ plugin, and Python-exposed function calls as deterministic runtime actions with declared inputs, side effects, and required evidence.
physical_assumptions: Blueprint or C++ function calls are part of the executable physics program, not informal implementation details.
Calls that mutate physics state must be ordered, replayable, and visible in engine-state logs.
Runtime actor placement should determine which actors receive mesh, material, collision, mass, velocity, impulse, and capture-driver calls.
required_assets: ADPPhysicsRuntime plugin or equivalent contact/trajectory exporter
asset registry with UE package paths
verifier_rules: Every physics-mutating function call references a known runtime_actor_id.
Mass, collider, collision profile, material, transform, velocity, impulse, and capture calls are echoed in engine-state logs.
No passive body receives hidden velocity or impulse before the declared action/contact/release event.
Contact and trajectory export functions run before the verifier marks a UE run reference-ready.
repair_suggestions: Add blueprint_call_plan to the runtime scene when a case requires Blueprint/C++ side effects.
Expose missing Blueprint or C++ function calls through the local UE runner instead of hard-coding case behavior.
Record function-call echo artifacts before trusting trajectory or contact outputs.
